title: 1. Variables & Basic Types
code:
# Integer, Float, String, Boolean
x = 10
y = 3.14
name = "Python"
is_active = True

# Type conversion
str_x = str(x)
int_y = int(y)

title: 2. Math Operations
code:
a = 10
b = 3

add = a + b      # 13
sub = a - b      # 7
mul = a * b      # 30
div = a / b      # 3.333...
flor = a // b    # 3 (Floor division)
mod = a % b      # 1 (Modulo)
exp = a ** b     # 1000 (Exponentiation)

title: 3. Strings & Formatting
code:
name = "Alice"
age = 25

# f-Strings (Python 3.6+)
msg = f"{name} is {age} years old."

# String Methods
"hello".capitalize() # 'Hello'
"hello".upper()      # 'HELLO'
"  hello  ".strip()   # 'hello'
"a,b,c".split(",")   # ['a', 'b', 'c']
"-".join(['a', 'b']) # 'a-b'

title: 4. Lists (Mutable)
code:
nums = [1, 2, 3]

nums.append(4)       # [1, 2, 3, 4]
nums.insert(0, 0)    # [0, 1, 2, 3, 4]
nums.pop()           # Removes last item
nums.remove(2)       # Removes first '2'
nums.sort()          # Sorts in-place

# Slicing
nums[1:3]            # Get items from idx 1 to 2
nums[::-1]           # Reverse list

title: 5. Tuples (Immutable)
code:
coords = (10, 20)

# Accessing elements
x = coords[0]

# Unpacking
x, y = coords

# Note: coords[0] = 15 will throw an error!
# Tuples cannot be modified after creation.

title: 6. Dictionaries
code:
user = {"name": "Alice", "age": 25}

user["city"] = "NY"   # Add/Update
user.get("job", "NA") # Safe access (returns 'NA' if not found)

# Looping
for key, val in user.items():
    print(f"{key}: {val}")

user.keys()   # dict_keys(['name', 'age'])
user.values() # dict_values(['Alice', 25])

title: 7. Sets (Unique)
8. Conditionals
9. Loops
code:
# For Loop
for i in range(3):
    print(i)  # 0, 1, 2

# While Loop
count = 0
while count < 3:
    count += 1

# Loop Control
for i in range(5):
    if i == 2: continue # Skips 2
    if i == 4: break    # Stops loop

# Enumerate
for idx, val in enumerate(["A", "B"]):
    print(idx, val)

title: 10. Functions
11. *args & **kwargs
12. Lambda Functions
code:
# Anonymous functions (single expression)
add = lambda x, y: x + y
print(add(5, 3)) # 8

# Often used with map/filter/sorted
nums = [1, 2, 3, 4]
squares = list(map(lambda x: x**2, nums))
evens = list(filter(lambda x: x % 2 == 0, nums))

# Sorting by specific key
users = [{"name": "Bob", "age": 30}, {"name": "Alice", "age": 25}]
users.sort(key=lambda u: u["age"])

title: 13. Comprehensions
14. Classes & OOP
code:
class Dog:
    # Class Attribute
    species = "Canis familiaris"

    # Constructor
    def __init__(self, name, age):
        self.name = name  # Instance Attribute
        self.age = age
        
    # Instance Method
    def bark(self):
        return f"{self.name} says Woof!"

my_dog = Dog("Rex", 3)
print(my_dog.bark())

title: 15. Inheritance & Magic Methods
16. Exception Handling
17. File Handling
code:
# Context Managers (with statement) automatically close files

# Writing to a file
with open("data.txt", "w") as f:
    f.write("Hello Python")

# Reading from a file
with open("data.txt", "r") as f:
    content = f.read()
    
# Reading line by line
with open("data.txt", "r") as f:
    for line in f:
        print(line.strip())

title: 18. Scope & Global
code:
x = 10 # Global scope

def modify():
    global x
    x = 20 # Modifies the global x
    
def outer():
    y = 5
    def inner():
        nonlocal y
        y = 10 # Modifies outer's y
    inner()
    print(y) # 10

title: 19. Iterators & Generators
code:
# Generator function uses 'yield' instead of 'return'
def count_up_to(max_val):
    count = 1
    while count <= max_val:
        yield count
        count += 1

counter = count_up_to(3)
print(next(counter)) # 1
print(next(counter)) # 2

# Memory efficient for large datasets
for num in count_up_to(1000000):
    pass

title: 20. Decorators
21. Modules & Imports
code:
# Import entire module
import math
print(math.pi)

# Import specific functions
from datetime import datetime
print(datetime.now())

# Alias
import numpy as np

# Custom Modules
# If you have 'utils.py', you can do:
# import utils
# utils.my_function()

title: 22. JSON Handling
code:
import json

data = {"name": "Alice", "age": 25}

# Dictionary to JSON String
json_str = json.dumps(data)

# JSON String to Dictionary
parsed_data = json.loads(json_str)

# Write JSON to file
with open('data.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(data, f)
    
# Read JSON from file
with open('data.json', 'r') as f:
    loaded = json.load(f)

title: 23. Collections Module
code:
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

# Counter (Count occurrences)
counts = Counter(['a', 'b', 'a', 'c', 'b', 'a'])
print(counts) # Counter({'a': 3, 'b': 2, 'c': 1})

# DefaultDict (Avoid KeyError)
d = defaultdict(int) # Default value is 0
d['missing_key'] += 1
print(d['missing_key']) # 1

title: 24. Regular Expressions (re)
code:
import re

text = "Contact me at alice@email.com or bob@email.com"

# Find all matches
emails = re.findall(r'\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,}\b', text)
print(emails)

# Substitution
redacted = re.sub(r'\d{4}-\d{4}', 'XXXX-XXXX', "Card: 1234-5678")

title: 25. Datetime Module
code:
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

now = datetime.now()
print(now.strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))

# Parse string to datetime
dt = datetime.strptime("2023-01-01", "%Y-%m-%d")

# Time math
tomorrow = now + timedelta(days=1)
next_week = now + timedelta(weeks=1)

python -m venv venv

venv\Scripts\activate

# Activate (Mac/Linux)
source venv/bin/activate

# Deactivate
deactivate

title: 28. Pip & Dependencies
code:
# Terminal / Command Line

# Install a package
pip install requests

# Install specific version
pip install Flask==2.0.1

# Save dependencies to file
pip freeze > requirements.txt

# Install from requirements file
pip install -r requirements.txt

title: 29. Asyncio (Basics)
code:
import asyncio

async def fetch_data():
    print("Fetching...")
    await asyncio.sleep(2) # Simulate I/O bound wait
    return {"data": 123}

async def main():
    # Run fetch_data
    result = await fetch_data()
    print(result)

# To run the async function
if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())

title: 30. Making API Requests
code:
import requests

# GET Request
response = requests.get("https://api.github.com")
if response.status_code == 200:
    data = response.json()
    print(data)

# POST Request
payload = {"key": "value"}
res = requests.post("https://httpbin.org/post", json=payload)
print(res.json())
